questions.question: What specific capability makes a 'BranchPythonOperator' in Airflow particularly valuable in an automated ML training pipeline?
options: It runs Python scripts in parallel across 10 Airflow workers simultaneously.

It enables conditional logic, allowing the pipeline to automatically evaluate whether the newly trained model outperforms the current production model and then dynamically branch — either proceeding to deployment or routing to a 'reject and alert' path.
It integrates Airflow with Kubernetes GPU nodes automatically.
It compresses trained model weights before uploading to S3.
questions.answer: It enables conditional logic, allowing the pipeline to automatically evaluate whether the newly trained model outperforms the current production model and then dynamically branch — either proceeding to deployment or routing to a 'reject and alert' path.
questions.explanation: An automated ML pipeline should never blindly deploy a newly trained model. The BranchPythonOperator gates deployment on objective performance criteria.

questions.question: What is the key architectural advantage of Kubeflow Pipelines over standard Apache Airflow for ML-specific workloads?
options: Kubeflow Pipelines does not require Kubernetes to run.

Each Kubeflow pipeline step runs in its own isolated Docker container with a completely independent environment, natively enabling GPU attachment, framework-specific libraries, and Kubernetes-native autoscaling — capabilities that Airflow's standard Python workers don't easily provide.
Kubeflow is significantly cheaper to operate than Airflow.
Kubeflow Pipelines has a built-in Feature Store.
questions.answer: Each Kubeflow pipeline step runs in its own isolated Docker container with a completely independent environment, natively enabling GPU attachment, framework-specific libraries, and Kubernetes-native autoscaling — capabilities that Airflow's standard Python workers don't easily provide.
questions.explanation: You can't easily attach an NVIDIA A100 GPU to a standard Airflow PythonOperator. With Kubeflow, you declaratively assign a GPU node to the training step container.

questions.question: What critical role does an 'Experiment Tracker' (like MLflow) play in a production ML Pipeline?
options: It tracks the cost of Airflow DAG runs.

It automatically records every training run's parameters (hyperparameters, dataset version, code commit SHA) and metrics (accuracy, AUC, F1), creating a complete, auditable history that enables comparing experiments and reproducing any past model exactly.
It monitors the Kafka cluster's consumer lag in real-time.
It stores the raw training dataset in a columnar format.
questions.answer: It automatically records every training run's parameters (hyperparameters, dataset version, code commit SHA) and metrics (accuracy, AUC, F1), creating a complete, auditable history that enables comparing experiments and reproducing any past model exactly.
questions.explanation: Without an experiment tracker, 'which hyperparameters gave us that 94% accuracy last Tuesday?' is an unanswerable question. MLflow records everything automatically.
